summary: Charity Majors shares her hard-won process for making boba (tapioca pearls) at home after months of trial and error during quarantine. The post is a practical cooking guide born from her ADHD-driven food fixation on boba, covering sourcing safe ingredients, avoiding ruined pans, and achieving the perfect chewy texture.
key_points: ADHD can manifest as intense food fixations lasting months to years
Many cheap boba brands from Amazon made her ill - sourcing matters
The key to not ruining pans is using a massive amount of water relative to boba
Boba requires a long cook (20-25 min boiling + 20-25 min resting) and doesn't keep well
money_quotes: Last year I was diagnosed with ADHD, which was a great surprise to me (if no one else).
One year it was manhattans (makers mark, sweet vermouth and bitters) and I seriously worried I was becoming an alcoholic.

I finally figured out how to make my own boba. This was NONTRIVIAL. It took the sacrifice of countless pans and far too many nights doubled over with nausea and stomach cramps, and months of trial and error.
The tapioca dust turns to gummy that sticks to the sides and bottom and gets baked on like a motherfucker.
key_insight: This is a personal lifestyle post about making boba at home, revealing Charity's characteristic voice and humor outside her usual tech topics, driven by her ADHD tendency to fixate intensely on one thing at a time.
